name: aws
description: Amazon Web Services command-line interface, version 2.

installs.notes: Multi-step: download the zip (swap x86_64 for aarch64 on ARM), `unzip -o /tmp/awscliv2.zip -d /tmp`, then `sudo /tmp/aws/install`. The installer itself needs sudo because it writes to /usr/local. `unzip` may need installing first.
Installs v1, not v2 — only offer if the user explicitly wants pip. ~/.local/bin must be on PATH.

commonFailures: pip-installed v1 shadows or conflicts with a v2 install; `aws --version` disambiguates.
`aws: command not found` after the official installer usually means /usr/local/bin is missing from PATH (common in minimal containers).
Installed but every call fails with credential errors — `aws configure` (or SSO setup) is a required post-install step Sprout should mention, not perform.
